pattern: Count element frequencies with a hash map, then scan for values whose frequency matches the value itself, returning the largest such value.
hints: You need to know how many times each integer appears — what structure gives you that in one pass?
After building the frequency map, iterate over its entries and collect every key whose count equals the key itself.
There may be multiple lucky integers; take the maximum. If the set of lucky integers is empty, the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: single element — value 2, frequency 1, not lucky
multiple lucky integers — return the largest
no element has frequency equal to its value
all elements identical and count equals value
element with value equal to array length — a trap for off-by-one
value appears more times than itself — not lucky
value 500 appears 500 times — max boundary lucky integer
two candidates; pick max, not first encountered

stress.description: 500 random integers in [1, 500] — may or may not contain lucky integers
